Counterfactual fairness

Counterfactual fairness
link: https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2017/file/a486cd07e4ac3d270571622f4f316ec5-Paper.pdf

Definitions:

defs

AAA: Protected attributes, sensitive features
XXX: features of individuals, excluding A
UUU: latent features not observed, represented
YYY: predictor

Fairness through unawareness (FTU):

An algorithm is fair so long as any protected attributes AAA are not explicitly used in the decision-making process.
Shortcoming: XXX might intersects AAA

Individual Fairness (IF).

For distance metric(should be carefully choosen), d(⋅,⋅)d(\cdot , \cdot)d(⋅,⋅), if d(i,j)d(i, j)d(i,j) is small, then Y^(X(i),A(i))≈Y^(X(j),A(j))\hat Y(X^{(i)}, A^{(i)}) \approx \hat Y(X^{(j)}, A^{(j)})Y^(X(i),A(i))≈Y^(X(j),A(j))

Demographic Parity (DP)(人口统计学意义上的平等)

Predictor Y^\hat YY^ satisfies demographic partiy if P(Y^∣A=0)=P(Y^∣A=1)P(\hat Y|A=0)=P(\hat Y|A=1)P(Y^∣A=0)=P(Y^∣A=1)

Equality of Opportunity

P(Y^∣A=0,Y=1)=P(Y^∣A=1,Y=1)P(\hat Y|A=0, Y=1)=P(\hat Y|A=1, Y=1)P(Y^∣A=0,Y=1)=P(Y^∣A=1,Y=1)

Causal Models(因果推断), Counterfacutal、

Casual Model (U,V,F)(U, V, F)(U,V,F),
UUU: latent background variables,
VVV: observed variables,
F={f1.f2,⋯ ,fn}F=\{f_1. f_2, \cdots, f_n\}F={f1​.f2​,⋯,fn​}, for each Vi=fi(pai,Upai)∈V,pai⊆V\ViV_i=f_i(pa_i, U_{pa_i})\in V, pa_i \subseteq V \backslash {V_i}Vi​=fi​(pai​,Upai​​)∈V,pai​⊆V\Vi​

Three Steps of Inference\

  • Abduction：for a given prior on UUU, compute the posterior distribution of UUU given the evidence W=wW = wW=w
  • Action：substitute the equations for ZZZ with the interventional values zzz, resulting in the modified set of equations FzF_zFz​
  • Prediction:

FairGAD

https://openreview.net/forum?id=3cE6NKYy8x

https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.04937

Fair GAD problem

GAD
G=(V,E,X)G=(V, E, X)G=(V,E,X),
node feature matrix X∈Rn×dX\in \R^{n\times d}X∈Rn×d,
Adjacency matrix A∈{0,1}n×nA\in \{0,1\}^{n\times n}A∈{0,1}n×n,
Anomaly labels Y∈{0,1}nY\in \{0, 1\}^nY∈{0,1}n, predicted Y^\hat YY^,
Fair GAD
sensitive attributes S∈{0,1}nS\in \{0, 1\}^nS∈{0,1}n, a binary feature XXX.
Performance matrix: accuracy and AUCROC: Area under the ROC Curve
Unfairness Mextrics, Statistic Parity(SP):SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0)−P(Y^=1∣S=1)∣SP = |P(\hat Y=1|S=0)−P(\hat Y =1|S=1)|SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0)−P(Y^=1∣S=1)∣,
Equality of Odds (EOO): SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0,Y=1)−P(Y^=1∣S=1,Y=1)∣SP = |P(\hat Y=1|S=0, Y=1)−P(\hat Y =1|S=1, Y=1)|SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0,Y=1)−P(Y^=1∣S=1,Y=1)∣

Data

  • Reddit:
    graph structure： linking two user posted the name subreddit within 24h.
    Node feature: Embedding from post histories.
  • Twitter:
    graph structure:: A follows B.
    Node feature: demographic infromation using M3 system, multimodal, multilingual, multi attirbute demographix inderence framework.

2024 Counterfactual Learning on Graphs: A Survey

3.5.1 How to create synthetic dataset

2022 Learning Fair Node Representations with Graph Counter factual Fairness

Two limitation on existing CF on graph:

  1. SiS_iSi​ affect the predetection. Red
  2. SiS_iSi​ affect A,XiA, X_iA,Xi​ Green

GEAR: Graph Counterfactually Fair Node Representation

  1. subgraph generation
    Node Importance Score by prune range of casualmodel to ego-centric subgraph( node and its neighbour)
  2. Counterfactual Data Argmentation:
    Graph Auto encodder and fair contrains: self-pertubation(flip its SiS_iSi​), neighbour pertubatiob
  3. Node Representation Learning :
    Siamese network to minimize discrepancy

Def, Graph conterfactual fairness:
An encoder Φ(⋅)\Phi(\cdot)Φ(⋅) satisfies graph counterfactual fairness if for any node iii:

P((Zi)S←s′∣X=X,A=A)=P((Zi)S←s′′∣X=X,A=A),P((Z_i)_{S \leftarrow s'} | X = \mathbf{X}, A = \mathbf{A}) = P((Z_i)_{S \leftarrow s''} | X = \mathbf{X}, A = \mathbf{A}), P((Zi​)S←s′​∣X=X,A=A)=P((Zi​)S←s′′​∣X=X,A=A),

for all s′≠s′′s' \neq s''s′​=s′′, where s′,s′′∈{0,1}ns', s'' \in \{0, 1\}^ns′,s′′∈{0,1}n are arbitrary sensitive attribute values of all nodes, Zi=(Φ(X,A))iZ_i = (\Phi(\mathbf{X}, \mathbf{A}))_iZi​=(Φ(X,A))i​ denotes the node representations.

Φ\PhiΦ, minimize the discrepancy between representation Φ(XS←s′,AS←s′)\Phi(X_{S\leftarrow s'}, A_{S\leftarrow s'})Φ(XS←s′​,AS←s′​) and Φ(XS←s′′,AS←s′′)\Phi(X_{S\leftarrow s''}, A_{S\leftarrow s''})Φ(XS←s′′​,AS←s′′​)

GEAR

1) subgraph generation

Personalized Pagerank algorithm:
Importance score R=α(I−(1−αAˉ))\mathbf R=\alpha (\mathbf I-(1-\alpha \mathbf {\bar A}))R=α(I−(1−αAˉ)), I\mathbf II, identity
Ri,jR_{i,j}Ri,j​ How node jjj is important for node iii, α∈[0,1]\alpha \in [0,1]α∈[0,1]

column-normalized adjacency matric, D:Di,i=∑jAi,j\mathbf D: \mathbf D_{i, i}=\sum_j A{i, j}D:Di,i​=∑j​Ai,j

G(i)=Sub(i,G,k)\mathcal{G}^{(i)}=Sub(i, \mathcal{G}, k)G(i)=Sub(i,G,k) :, subgraph generation

  • G(i)={V(i),E(i),X(i)}={A(i),X(i)},\mathcal{G}^{(i)} = \{ \mathcal{V}^{(i)}, \mathcal{E}^{(i)}, \mathbf{X}^{(i)} \} = \{ \mathbf{A}^{(i)}, \mathbf{X}^{(i)} \}, G(i)={V(i),E(i),X(i)}={A(i),X(i)}, Vertive, Edge, Features with includes in XXX, and , where

  • V(i)=TOP(Ri,:,k),\mathcal{V}^{(i)} = \text{TOP}(\mathbf{R}_{i,:}, k),V(i)=TOP(Ri,:​,k),

  • A(i)=AV(i),V(i),X(i)=XV(i),:,\mathbf{A}^{(i)} = \mathbf{A}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}, \mathcal{V}^{(i)}}, \quad \mathbf{X}^{(i)} = \mathbf{X}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}, :}, A(i)=AV(i),V(i)​,X(i)=XV(i),:​,,

2）Counterfactual Data Augmentation

GraphVAG: graph variational auto-encoder
latent embedding H={h1,h2,⋯ ,hk}H=\{h_1, h_2, \cdots, h_k\}H={h1​,h2​,⋯,hk​} HHH is sampled from q(H∣X,A)q(H|X, A)q(H∣X,A), p(𝐻)p(𝐻)p(H) is a standard Normal prior distribution
L=\mathcal{L}=L=

s~i\tilde{s}_is~i​: summary of neighbor info, aggregationof all nodes in subgarph G(i)\mathcal{G}^{(i)}G(i)
s~i=1∣V(i)∣∑j∈V(i)sj\tilde{s}_i = \frac{1}{|\mathcal{V}^{(i)}|} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{V}^{(i)}} s_js~i​=∣V(i)∣1​∑j∈V(i)​sj​

Discriminator,D(⋅)D(\cdot)D(⋅)
D(H,b)D(\mathbf{H}, b)D(H,b) predicts the probability of whether the summary of sensitive attribute values is in range bbb

Fairness Constraint
Ld=∑b∈BE[log⁡(D(H,b))]L_d = \sum_{b \in B} \mathbb{E} [\log(D(\mathbf{H}, b))]Ld​=∑b∈B​E[log(D(H,b))]
LdL_dLd​ is a regularizer to minimize the mutual information between the summary of sensitive attribute values and the
embeddings

Final Loss for Counterfactual Data Augmentation
La=Lr+βLdL_a = L_r + \beta L_dLa​=Lr​+βLd​
β\betaβ is a hyperparameter for the weight of fairness constraint
Use alternating SGD for optimization:

  1. minimize LaL_{a}La​ by fixing the discriminator and updating parameters in other parts;
  2. minimize −La−L_{a}−La​ with respect to the discriminator while other parts fixed.

Self-Perturbation

G‾(i)={GSi←1−si(i)}\overline{\mathcal{G}}^{(i)} = \{ \mathcal{G}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow 1-s_i} \}G​(i)={GSi​←1−si​(i)​} (flipping sensitive feature)

Neighbor-Perturbation

G‾(i)={GS∖i(i)←SMP(SV∖i(i)(i))(i)}\underline{\mathcal{G}}^{(i)} = \left\{ \mathcal{G}^{(i)}_{S^{(i)}_{\setminus i} \leftarrow \text{SMP}(S^{(i)}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}})} \right\}G​(i)=⎩⎪⎨⎪⎧​GS∖i(i)​←SMP(SV∖i(i)​(i)​)(i)​⎭⎪⎬⎪⎫​

subgraph G(i)\mathcal{G}^{(i)}G(i) ego(iii)-center subgraph with noes V(i)\mathcal{V}^{(i)}V(i), exclude node iii: V∖i(i)\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}V∖i(i)​, randomly preterbe the sentsitice value of other nodes: SMP(V∖i(i))SMP(\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i})SMP(V∖i(i)​)

Reconstruction Loss (GraphVAE Module)
Lr=Eq(H∣X,A)[−log⁡(p(X,A∣H,S))]+KL[q(H∣X,A)∥p(H)]L_r = \mathbb{E}_{q(\mathbf{H}|X, A)} \left[ -\log(p(X, A | \mathbf{H}, S)) \right] + \text{KL}[q(\mathbf{H} | X, A) \| p(\mathbf{H})]Lr​=Eq(H∣X,A)​[−log(p(X,A∣H,S))]+KL[q(H∣X,A)∥p(H)]

3) Fair Representation learning

Fairness Loss
Lf=1∣V∣∑i∈V((1−λs)d(zi,zˉi)+λsd(zi,z‾i)), L_f = \frac{1}{|\mathcal{V}|} \sum_{i \in \mathcal{V}} \left( (1 - \lambda_s) d(z_i, \bar{z}_i) + \lambda_s d(z_i, \underline{z}_i) \right), Lf​=∣V∣1​∑i∈V​((1−λs​)d(zi​,zˉi​)+λs​d(zi​,z​i​)),
λs\lambda_sλs​ hyperparam control neig-preturbation weight

Node Representations

  • zi=(ϕ(X(i),A(i)))i, z_i = (\phi(\mathbf{X}^{(i)}, \mathbf{A}^{(i)}))_i, zi​=(ϕ(X(i),A(i)))i​,
  • zˉi=AGG({(ϕ(XSi←1−si(i),ASi←1−si(i)))i}), \bar{z}_i = \text{AGG} \left( \left\{ (\phi(\mathbf{X}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow 1-s_i}, \mathbf{A}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow 1-s_i}))_i \right\} \right), zˉi​=AGG({(ϕ(XSi​←1−si​(i)​,ASi​←1−si​(i)​))i​}),
  • z‾i=AGG({(ϕ(XSi←SMP(SV∖i(i)(i))(i),ASi←SMP(SV∖i(i)(i))(i))i}), \underline{z}_i = \text{AGG} \left( \left\{ (\phi(\mathbf{X}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow \text{SMP}(S^{(i)}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}})}, \mathbf{A}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow \text{SMP}(S^{(i)}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}})})_i \right\} \right), z​i​=AGG⎝⎜⎛​⎩⎪⎨⎪⎧​(ϕ(XSi​←SMP(SV∖i(i)​(i)​)(i)​,ASi​←SMP(SV∖i(i)​(i)​)(i)​)i​⎭⎪⎬⎪⎫​⎠⎟⎞​,

Prediction Loss
Lp=1n∑i∈[n]l(f(zi),yi),L_p = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i \in [n]} l(f(z_i), y_i),Lp​=n1​∑i∈[n]​l(f(zi​),yi​), lll: could be CE(Cross entropy), f(⋅)f(\cdot)f(⋅) makes predictions for downstream tasks with the representations, i.e.

Overall Loss
L=Lp+λLf+μ∥θ∥2, L = L_p + \lambda L_f + \mu \| \theta \|^2, L=Lp​+λLf​+μ∥θ∥2,

Dataset creation

Sensitive Attributes
Si∼Bernoulli(p),S_i \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p),Si​∼Bernoulli(p), p=0.4p=0.4p=0.4 percent Si=1S_i=1Si​=1

Latent Embeddings
Zi∼N(0,I),Z_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I}),Zi​∼N(0,I),
I\mathbf{I}I identity, dimension of ZiZ_iZi​: ds=50d_s=50ds​=50

Node Features
Xi=S(Zi)+Siv,X_i = \mathcal{S}(Z_i) + S_i \mathbf{v},Xi​=S(Zi​)+Si​v,
sampling operation S(⋅)S(\cdot)S(⋅) select 25 dims from ZiZ_iZi​, v∼N(0,I)\mathbf{v} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I})v∼N(0,I)

Graph Structure
P(Ai,j=1)=σ(cos(Zi,Zj)+a1(Si=Sj)),P(A_{i,j} = 1) = \sigma(\text{cos}(Z_i, Z_j) + a \mathbf{1}(S_i = S_j)),P(Ai,j​=1)=σ(cos(Zi​,Zj​)+a1(Si​=Sj​)),
σ\sigmaσ sigmoid function, 1(Si=Sj)==Si=Sj.α=0.01\mathbf{1}(S_i = S_j)==S_i = S_j. \alpha=0.011(Si​=Sj​)==Si​=Sj​.α=0.01

Node Labels
Yi=B(wZi+ws∑j∈NiSj∣Ni∣),Y_i = \mathcal{B}(w Z_i + w_s \frac{\sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}_i} S_j}{|\mathcal{N}_i|}),Yi​=B(wZi​+ws​∣Ni​∣∑j∈Ni​​Sj​​),
B\mathcal{B}B Bernulli distribution,Ni\mathcal{N}_iNi​ set of neighbors of node i w,wiw, w_iw,wi​ weight vector

Result

Using Synthetic dataset, Bail, Credit

24 Three Revisits to Node-Level Graph Anomaly Detection

Outliers, Message Passing and Hyperbolic Neural Networks

Previous Outlier injection method

G=(V,E,X,y)\mathcal{G}=(\mathcal{V}, \mathcal{E}, X, y)G=(V,E,X,y): vertice set, edge set, attibute matrix, label of class

  • Contextual(cntxt.) outlier injection
    Normalize features xi′=xi∣∣xi∣∣1x_i'=\frac{x_i}{||x_i||_1}xi′​=∣∣xi​∣∣1​xi​​
    Sample ooo nodes from V\mathcal{V}V as Vc\mathcal{V}_cVc​. without replacement
    For node iii in Vc\mathcal{V}_cVc​, sample qqq nodes from Vr=V−Vc\mathcal{V}_r=\mathcal{V}- \mathcal{V}_cVr​=V−Vc​, among them choose the farthest one j=argmaxk(∣∣xi′−xk′∣∣2)j = \text{argmax}_k(||x_i'-x_k'||_2)j=argmaxk​(∣∣xi′​−xk′​∣∣2​) to replace xix_ixi​ with xjx_jxj​.

  • Strctural(stct.) outlier injection
    create ttt groups sized sss with anomalous nodes.
    sample o=t×so=t\times so=t×s from V\mathcal{V}V without replacement
    Then randoms partition into ttt groups.
    Add edges to make them a clique(fully connected), then drop edges with ppp probability

Score function

The farthest node will have large ∣∣x~i∣∣2||\tilde{\mathbf x}_i||_2∣∣x~i​∣∣2​
A structural outlier node iii will have many neighbors leads to large ∣∣a~i∣∣1||\tilde{\mathbf a}_i||_1∣∣a~i​∣∣1​

Score function: scorenorm(i)=α∣∣x~i∣∣2+(1−α)∣∣a~i∣∣1score_{norm}(i)=\alpha||\tilde{\mathbf x}_i||_2+(1-\alpha)||\tilde {\mathbf a}_i||_1scorenorm​(i)=α∣∣x~i​∣∣2​+(1−α)∣∣a~i​∣∣1​, x~i\tilde{\mathbf x}_ix~i​: xix_ixi​ after outlier injection, a~i\tilde{\mathbf a}_ia~i​: aia_iai​ after outlier injection, Aii=1A_{ii}=1Aii​=1
where cntxt OD, α=1\alpha=1α=1, stct OD, α=0\alpha=0α=0 : α\alphaα ratio of two methods

test 1: ROC-AUC
For each dataset, use original dataset v.s. l2-nrom for each xix_ixi​
do anomaly injection. apply GAD Method to get scorenormscore_{norm}scorenorm​

Novel Anomaly injection method

Sum in terms of Dataset

从数据集的角度来说：

FairGAD:

Reddit:

  • 数据来源：Post on politic related subReddit
  • Labelling Y: based on FACTOID(Sakketou et al., 2022), use the num of posted link(left or right)
  • Graph construciton:

CaD-VAE

Causal Disentangled Variational Auto-Encoder for Preference Understanding in Recommendation
Link: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2304.07922

Challenges: inability to disentangle the latent factor
DLR: Disentangled Representation learning
- DEAR: (Disentangled gEnerative cAusal Representation (DEAR)) https://arxiv.org/abs/2010.02637
- CasualVAE: https://doi.org/10.1109/CVPR46437.2021.00947

In Casual Layer: The SCM is

2.1

  • u∈{1,…,U}u \in \{1, \ldots, U\}u∈{1,…,U}: user index

  • i∈{1,…,I}i \in \{1, \ldots, I\}i∈{1,…,I}: item index

  • D=(U,I,X)\mathcal{D}=(U, I, X)D=(U,I,X): dataset

    • For a user uuu, the historical interactions Du={xu,i:xu,i∈{0,1}}D_u = \{x_{u,i} : x_{u,i} \in \{0,1\}\}Du​={xu,i​:xu,i​∈{0,1}} form a multi-hot vector.
    • xu,i=0x_{u,i} = 0xu,i​=0 means no recorded interaction between user uuu and item iii.
    • xu,i=1x_{u,i} = 1xu,i​=1 means an interaction between user uuu and item iii, such as a click.

  • xux_uxu​ denotes all interactions of the user uuu:

xu={xu,i:xu,i=1}x_u = \{x_{u,i} : x_{u,i} = 1\} xu​={xu,i​:xu,i​=1}

  • Users may have diverse interests and interact with items that belong to many high-level concepts, such as preferred film directors, actors, genres, and year of production.

2.2

z=g((I−AT)−1ϵ):=Fα(ϵ)z = g \left( (I - A^T)^{-1} \epsilon \right) := F_\alpha (\epsilon) z=g((I−AT)−1ϵ):=Fα​(ϵ)

  • zzz: causal variable

  • ϵ\epsilonϵ: exogenous variables from a normal distribution - N(0,I)\mathcal{N}(0, I)N(0,I)

  • ggg: nonlinear element-wise transformations

  • α\alphaα: parameters (A,g)(A, g)(A,g).

  • AAA: weighted adjacency matrix: AijA_{ij}Aij​ is non-zero only if [z]i[z]_i[z]i​ is a parent of [z]j[z]_j[z]j​. The binary adjacency matrix IAI_AIA​ indicates where A≠0A \neq 0A​=0

  • To ensure disentanglement, labels of concepts ccc are used as additional information

    • If ggg is invertible, the equation can be rephrased as:

    gi−1(zi)=AiTgi−1(z)+ϵig_i^{-1}(z_i) = A_i^T g_i^{-1}(z) + \epsilon_i gi−1​(zi​)=AiT​gi−1​(z)+ϵi​

    This implies that after a nonlinear transformation ggg, the factors zzz satisfy a linear SCM.

  1. Generative Model Assumption:
    • For a user uuu, the generative model parameterized by θ\thetaθ assumes that the observed data are generated from the following distribution:

      pθ(xu)=Epθ(c)[∬pθ(xu∣ϵ,zu,c)pθ(ϵ,zu∣c)dϵdzu]p_\theta(x_u) = \mathbb{E}_{p_\theta(c)} \left[ \iint p_\theta (x_u | \epsilon, z_u, c) p_\theta (\epsilon, z_u | c) d\epsilon dz_u \right] pθ​(xu​)=Epθ​(c)​[∬pθ​(xu​∣ϵ,zu​,c)pθ​(ϵ,zu​∣c)dϵdzu​]

      Here, xux_uxu​ is the observed data for user uuu, ϵ\epsilonϵ are the exogenous variables, zuz_uzu​ are the latent variables, and ccc are the labels of the concepts.

GUIDE

  • Paper:
    https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/stamp/stamp.jsp?tp=&arnumber=9671990

EncoderResidual Attention Layer

Attribute: 就是普通的X
Encoder用三层GCN。

https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/30524
Counterfactual Graph Learning for Anomaly Detection with Feature
Disentanglement and Generation (Student Abstract)

NIFTY 21

  • paper: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.13186
  • Code: https://github.com/chirag126/nifty?tab=readme-ov-file

Augmented:

  • Node level
    • attribute masking r∼B(Pn)r \sim \mathcal{B}(P_n)r∼B(Pn​)
    • x~u=xu+r∘δ\tilde{\mathbf{x}}_u = \mathbf{x}_u + \mathbf{r} \circ \deltax~u​=xu​+r∘δ, where δ∈RM\delta \in \mathbb{R}^Mδ∈RM is sampled from a normal distribution.

  • sens attribute level

  • edge level

DEFEND

GEAR 22

c

MCCNIFTY 21

Fairness-Aware 21

CAF 23

paper: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2307.04937
code: https://github.com/TimeLovercc/CAF-GNN?tab=readme-ov-file

FairGNN

uses adversarial training to achieve fairness on graphs. It trains the learned representation via an adversary which is optimized to predict the sensitive attribute

EDITS 23

is a pre-processing method for fair graph learning. It aims to debias the input network to remove the sensitive
information in the graph data

Fatra 24

CAGAD 24

https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/stamp/stamp.jsp?tp=&arnumber=10564850
heterophily dominant neighbors: most of its neighbors have different class labels from the target node

  1. GPNN graph pointer nn: detect heter nodes
    composed of encoder and decoder
  2. DDMP (deniosing difussion probabilistic model): translate, create anomaly neigbors for heter nodes
  3. GAT Graph attention network: detect anomaly nodes
    有点想加一个PRAUC的测试指标： 所以当我们希望模型在正负样本上都能表现较好时使用 ROC-AUC 衡量，如果我们只关注模型对正样本的分辨能力使用 PR-AUC 更好

GFCN 24

https://arxiv.org/abs/2010.10274

GAD-NR 24 (in Pygod)

GAD with node rep learn

a survey on GAD -21

method and datasets
https://github.com/XiaoxiaoMa-MQ/Awesome-Deep-Graph-Anomaly-Detection

a survey 23: Graph Learning for Anomaly Analytics: Algorithms, Applications, and Challenges

https://dl.acm.org/doi/full/10.1145/3570906

ADA-GAD 24 AAAI（Anomaly-Denoised Autoencoders for Graph Anomaly Detection）

https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/28691

感觉最后写出来的应该类似是 Improving fairness for node-level GAE based GAD models via disentanglement learning

Domain Adaptation

属于transfer learning
2.2 GDA的两个用途：node/graph classification

  • U∈{S,T}\mathcal{U}\in\{S,T\}U∈{S,T} Domain
  • PU(X,Y)P_{\mathcal{U}}(X,Y)PU​(X,Y): joint feature and label distribution
  • {(xi,yi)}i=1N\{(x_i,y_i)\}_{i=1}^N{(xi​,yi​)}i=1N​: labeled source data
  • {(xi)}i=1M\{(x_i)\}_{i=1}^M{(xi​)}i=1M​: unlabeled target data IID sampled from the source and target domain respectively.
  • ϕ:X→H\phi:\mathcal{X}\rightarrow\mathcal{H}ϕ:X→H: a feature encoder
  • g:H→Yg:\mathcal{H}\rightarrow\mathcal{Y}g:H→Y: a classifier
  • ϵU(g∘ϕ)=PU(g(ϕ(X))≠Y)\epsilon_{\mathcal{U}}(g\circ\phi)=P_{\mathcal{U}}(g(\phi(X))\neq Y)ϵU​(g∘ϕ)=PU​(g(ϕ(X))​=Y):classification error in domain U\mathcal{U}U
  • The objective is to train the model with available data to minimize target error ϵT(g∘ϕ)\epsilon_T(g\circ\phi)ϵT​(g∘ϕ) when predicting target labels.

A popular DA strategy is to learn domain-invariant representation, ensuring similar PS(H)P_S(H)PS​(H) and PT(H)P_T(H)PT​(H) and minimizing the source error ϵS(g∘ϕ)\epsilon_S(g\circ\phi)ϵS​(g∘ϕ) to retain classification capability simultaneously (Zhao et al., 2019). This is achieved through

  • Feature Shift: PS(X∣Y)≠PT(X∣Y)P_S(X|Y) \neq P_T(X|Y)PS​(X∣Y)​=PT​(X∣Y)
    • Assume ndoe feature xux_uxu​，u∈Vu \in \mathcal{V}u∈V are IID sampled from P(X∣Y)P(X|Y)P(X∣Y). Therefore, P(X=x∣Y=y)=∏u∈VP(X=xu∣Y=yu)P(X = x|Y = y) = \prod_{u \in \mathcal{V}} P(X = x_u|Y = y_u)P(X=x∣Y=y)=∏u∈V​P(X=xu​∣Y=yu​)

Preassumption on model:

X←Y→AX\leftarrow Y \rightarrow AX←Y→A. Lables are generated first, then A and X are generated.

  • Strcture Shift: PS(A,Y)≠PT(A,Y)P_S(A, Y) \neq P_T(A, Y)PS​(A,Y)​=PT​(A,Y)
    • Given joint distribution of AAA, and node labels P(A,Y)P(A, Y)P(A,Y)

Preassumption:

  1. Model: X←Y→AX\leftarrow Y \rightarrow AX←Y→A. Lables are generated first, then A and X are generated.
  2. No Feature Shift: PS(X∣Y)=PT(X∣Y)P_S(X|Y) = P_T(X|Y)PS​(X∣Y)=PT​(X∣Y)

Structure Shift: PU(A,Y)=PU(A∣Y)PU(Y)P_{U}(A, Y) = P_{U}(A|Y)P_{U}(Y)PU​(A,Y)=PU​(A∣Y)PU​(Y)

  • Conditional Structure Shift: PS(A∣Y)≠PT(A∣Y)P_S(A|Y) \neq P_T(A|Y)PS​(A∣Y)​=PT​(A∣Y)
  • Label Shift: PS(Y)≠PT(Y)P_S(Y) \neq P_T(Y)PS​(Y)​=PT​(Y)

Because of the interconnected nature of graph data, the IID is not satisfied for strcture shift, and new alogrithm is needed for solving CSS.

structure shift is unique to graphs. In contrast to feature shift, which is analogous to non-IID feature shift in non-graph data, structure shift cannot be solved by adapting traditional conditional shift methods. Therefore, we assume feature shift is resolved, i.e., $P_S(X|Y) = P_T

Even if PS(H(k)∣Y)=PT(H(k)∣Y)P_S(H^{(k)}|Y) = P_T(H^{(k)}|Y)PS​(H(k)∣Y)=PT​(H(k)∣Y)
CSS may lead to PS(H(k+1)∣Y)≠PT(H(k+1)∣Y)P_S(H^{(k+1)}|Y) \neq P_T(H^{(k+1)}|Y)PS​(H(k+1)∣Y)​=PT​(H(k+1)∣Y)

GNN

hu(k+1)=UPT(hu(k),AGG({{hv(k):v∈Nu}}))h_u^{(k+1)} = \text{UPT}\left(h_u^{(k)}, \text{AGG}\left(\{\{h_v^{(k)} : v \in \mathcal{N}_u\}\}\right)\right) hu(k+1)​=UPT(hu(k)​,AGG({{hv(k)​:v∈Nu​}}))

  • {{⋅}}\{\{\cdot\}\}{{⋅}}: Multiset
  • hu(k+1)h_u^{(k+1)}hu(k+1)​: The updated representation of node uuu at layer k+1k+1k+1.
  • AGG(⋅)\text{AGG}(\cdot)AGG(⋅): Aggregates message from neighbors.
  • UPT(⋅)\text{UPT}(\cdot)UPT(⋅): Update function

Theorem 3.3 (Sufficient conditions for addressing CSS).

Given the following assumptions

  • Conditional Alignment in the previous layer k
    • PS(H(k)∣Y)=PT(H(k)∣Y)P_S(H^{(k)}|Y) = P_T(H^{(k)}|Y)PS​(H(k)∣Y)=PT​(H(k)∣Y) and ∀u∈Vu\forall u \in \mathcal{V}_u∀u∈Vu​, given Y=yuY = y_uY=yu​, hu(k)h_u^{(k)}hu(k)​ is independently sampled from PU(H(k)∣Y)P_{\mathcal{U}}(H^{(k)}|Y)PU​(H(k)∣Y).
  • Edge Conditional Independence
    • Given node labels yyy, edges mutually independently exist in the graph.

If there exists a transformation that modifies the neighborhood of node uuu: Nu→N~u,∀u∈VS\mathcal{N}_u \rightarrow \tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u, \forall u \in \mathcal{V}_SNu​→N~u​,∀u∈VS​, such that

  • PS(∣N~u∣∣Yu=i)=PT(∣N~u∣∣Yu=i)P_S(|\tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u||Y_u = i) = P_T(|\tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u||Y_u = i)PS​(∣N~u​∣∣Yu​=i)=PT​(∣N~u​∣∣Yu​=i)
  • PS(Yv∣Yu=i,v∈N~u)=PT(Yv∣Yu=i,v∈Nu),∀i,v∈YP_S(Y_v|Y_u = i, v \in \tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u) = P_T(Y_v|Y_u = i, v \in \mathcal{N}_u), \forall i, v \in \mathcal{Y}PS​(Yv​∣Yu​=i,v∈N~u​)=PT​(Yv​∣Yu​=i,v∈Nu​),∀i,v∈Y

then
PS(H(k+1)∣Y)=PT(H(k+1)∣Y) is satisfiedP_S(H^{(k+1)}|Y) = P_T(H^{(k+1)}|Y) \text{ is satisfied}PS​(H(k+1)∣Y)=PT​(H(k+1)∣Y) is satisfied

ϕγ\phi_\gammaϕγ​: GNN encoding with edge weight adjusting
ϕ\phiϕ: GNN encoding without adjusting
last-layer alignment PS(H(L)∣Y)=PT(H(L)∣Y)P_S(H^{(L)} \mid Y) = P_T(H^{(L)} \mid Y)PS​(H(L)∣Y)=PT​(H(L)∣Y)can be achieved with hS(L)=ϕγ(xS,AS)h_S^{(L)} = \phi_\gamma(x_S, A_S)hS(L)​=ϕγ​(xS​,AS​) and hT(L)=ϕ(xT,AT)h_T^{(L)} = \phi(x_T, A_T)hT(L)​=ϕ(xT​,AT​). Note that based on conditional alignment in the distribution of randomly sampled node representations PS(H(L)∣Y)=PT(H(L)∣Y)P_S(H^{(L)} \mid Y) = P_T(H^{(L)} \mid Y)PS​(H(L)∣Y)=PT​(H(L)∣Y) and under the conditions in Thm 3.3, PS(H(L)∣Y)=PT(H(L)∣Y)P_S(\mathbf{H}^{(L)} \mid Y) = P_T(\mathbf{H}^{(L)} \mid Y)PS​(H(L)∣Y)=PT​(H(L)∣Y) can also be achieved in the matrix form.

Gs=(As,Xs)G_s=(A_s,X_s)Gs​=(As​,Xs​)
Gt=(At,Xt)G_t=(A_t,X_t)Gt​=(At​,Xt​)
g∘ϕγg \circ \phi_\gammag∘ϕγ​
g∘ϕg \circ \phig∘ϕ
Y^s\hat Y_sY^s​
Y^t\hat Y_tY^t​

Drawback of StrucRW

  1. using www instead of γ\gammaγ to reweigt GsG_sGs​
  2. Rough estimation for www
  3. Not considering LS

LLM GAD

problem

GNN缺点：
GNN的message passing会导致N和A趋同，降低识别率
尽管在Heterophilic graph上有改进，但是没有改变single node rep的本质

method

(1) Sequence Construction
(2) Coherence-Aware Rep Computation
(3) Anomaly Detection via LLMs

text coherence
elvalueated by

  • llama 2: LLM model
  • LCD-G: cross-domain coherence eval od sentence

    48,509 normal sequences and 7,108 anomalous sequences

Sequence Construction

Multi sequences for each node by random walk (local)

  • starting from the target node (以target node为中点)
  • iteratively sampling neighboring nodes and their connecting edges. (h:点, e:边, hzhzhzh)

Coherence-Aware Rep Computation

micro

  • AGG info from edges within same sequence

macro

  • holistic edge information within the
    entire graph

AD via LLMs

comment:
可以解释一下为什么Multi-AD-MR表现差于ER吗
40% labeled？

Counterfactual fairness

Counterfactual fairness
link: https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2017/file/a486cd07e4ac3d270571622f4f316ec5-Paper.pdf

Definitions:

defs

AAA: Protected attributes, sensitive features
XXX: features of individuals, excluding A
UUU: latent features not observed, represented
YYY: predictor

Fairness through unawareness (FTU):

An algorithm is fair so long as any protected attributes AAA are not explicitly used in the decision-making process.
Shortcoming: XXX might intersects AAA

Individual Fairness (IF).

For distance metric(should be carefully choosen), d(⋅,⋅)d(\cdot , \cdot)d(⋅,⋅), if d(i,j)d(i, j)d(i,j) is small, then Y^(X(i),A(i))≈Y^(X(j),A(j))\hat Y(X^{(i)}, A^{(i)}) \approx \hat Y(X^{(j)}, A^{(j)})Y^(X(i),A(i))≈Y^(X(j),A(j))

Demographic Parity (DP)(人口统计学意义上的平等)

Predictor Y^\hat YY^ satisfies demographic partiy if P(Y^∣A=0)=P(Y^∣A=1)P(\hat Y|A=0)=P(\hat Y|A=1)P(Y^∣A=0)=P(Y^∣A=1)

Equality of Opportunity

P(Y^∣A=0,Y=1)=P(Y^∣A=1,Y=1)P(\hat Y|A=0, Y=1)=P(\hat Y|A=1, Y=1)P(Y^∣A=0,Y=1)=P(Y^∣A=1,Y=1)

Causal Models(因果推断), Counterfacutal、

Casual Model (U,V,F)(U, V, F)(U,V,F),
UUU: latent background variables,
VVV: observed variables,
F={f1.f2,⋯ ,fn}F=\{f_1. f_2, \cdots, f_n\}F={f1​.f2​,⋯,fn​}, for each Vi=fi(pai,Upai)∈V,pai⊆V\ViV_i=f_i(pa_i, U_{pa_i})\in V, pa_i \subseteq V \backslash {V_i}Vi​=fi​(pai​,Upai​​)∈V,pai​⊆V\Vi​

Three Steps of Inference\

  • Abduction：for a given prior on UUU, compute the posterior distribution of UUU given the evidence W=wW = wW=w
  • Action：substitute the equations for ZZZ with the interventional values zzz, resulting in the modified set of equations FzF_zFz​
  • Prediction:

FairGAD

https://openreview.net/forum?id=3cE6NKYy8x

https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.04937

Fair GAD problem

GAD
G=(V,E,X)G=(V, E, X)G=(V,E,X),
node feature matrix X∈Rn×dX\in \R^{n\times d}X∈Rn×d,
Adjacency matrix A∈{0,1}n×nA\in \{0,1\}^{n\times n}A∈{0,1}n×n,
Anomaly labels Y∈{0,1}nY\in \{0, 1\}^nY∈{0,1}n, predicted Y^\hat YY^,
Fair GAD
sensitive attributes S∈{0,1}nS\in \{0, 1\}^nS∈{0,1}n, a binary feature XXX.
Performance matrix: accuracy and AUCROC: Area under the ROC Curve
Unfairness Mextrics, Statistic Parity(SP):SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0)−P(Y^=1∣S=1)∣SP = |P(\hat Y=1|S=0)−P(\hat Y =1|S=1)|SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0)−P(Y^=1∣S=1)∣,
Equality of Odds (EOO): SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0,Y=1)−P(Y^=1∣S=1,Y=1)∣SP = |P(\hat Y=1|S=0, Y=1)−P(\hat Y =1|S=1, Y=1)|SP=∣P(Y^=1∣S=0,Y=1)−P(Y^=1∣S=1,Y=1)∣

Data

  • Reddit:
    graph structure： linking two user posted the name subreddit within 24h.
    Node feature: Embedding from post histories.
  • Twitter:
    graph structure:: A follows B.
    Node feature: demographic infromation using M3 system, multimodal, multilingual, multi attirbute demographix inderence framework.

2024 Counterfactual Learning on Graphs: A Survey

3.5.1 How to create synthetic dataset

2022 Learning Fair Node Representations with Graph Counter factual Fairness

Two limitation on existing CF on graph:

  1. SiS_iSi​ affect the predetection. Red
  2. SiS_iSi​ affect A,XiA, X_iA,Xi​ Green

GEAR: Graph Counterfactually Fair Node Representation

  1. subgraph generation
    Node Importance Score by prune range of casualmodel to ego-centric subgraph( node and its neighbour)
  2. Counterfactual Data Argmentation:
    Graph Auto encodder and fair contrains: self-pertubation(flip its SiS_iSi​), neighbour pertubatiob
  3. Node Representation Learning :
    Siamese network to minimize discrepancy

Def, Graph conterfactual fairness:
An encoder Φ(⋅)\Phi(\cdot)Φ(⋅) satisfies graph counterfactual fairness if for any node iii:

P((Zi)S←s′∣X=X,A=A)=P((Zi)S←s′′∣X=X,A=A),P((Z_i)_{S \leftarrow s'} | X = \mathbf{X}, A = \mathbf{A}) = P((Z_i)_{S \leftarrow s''} | X = \mathbf{X}, A = \mathbf{A}), P((Zi​)S←s′​∣X=X,A=A)=P((Zi​)S←s′′​∣X=X,A=A),

for all s′≠s′′s' \neq s''s′​=s′′, where s′,s′′∈{0,1}ns', s'' \in \{0, 1\}^ns′,s′′∈{0,1}n are arbitrary sensitive attribute values of all nodes, Zi=(Φ(X,A))iZ_i = (\Phi(\mathbf{X}, \mathbf{A}))_iZi​=(Φ(X,A))i​ denotes the node representations.

Φ\PhiΦ, minimize the discrepancy between representation Φ(XS←s′,AS←s′)\Phi(X_{S\leftarrow s'}, A_{S\leftarrow s'})Φ(XS←s′​,AS←s′​) and Φ(XS←s′′,AS←s′′)\Phi(X_{S\leftarrow s''}, A_{S\leftarrow s''})Φ(XS←s′′​,AS←s′′​)

GEAR

1) subgraph generation

Personalized Pagerank algorithm:
Importance score R=α(I−(1−αAˉ))\mathbf R=\alpha (\mathbf I-(1-\alpha \mathbf {\bar A}))R=α(I−(1−αAˉ)), I\mathbf II, identity
Ri,jR_{i,j}Ri,j​ How node jjj is important for node iii, α∈[0,1]\alpha \in [0,1]α∈[0,1]

column-normalized adjacency matric, D:Di,i=∑jAi,j\mathbf D: \mathbf D_{i, i}=\sum_j A{i, j}D:Di,i​=∑j​Ai,j

G(i)=Sub(i,G,k)\mathcal{G}^{(i)}=Sub(i, \mathcal{G}, k)G(i)=Sub(i,G,k) :, subgraph generation

  • G(i)={V(i),E(i),X(i)}={A(i),X(i)},\mathcal{G}^{(i)} = \{ \mathcal{V}^{(i)}, \mathcal{E}^{(i)}, \mathbf{X}^{(i)} \} = \{ \mathbf{A}^{(i)}, \mathbf{X}^{(i)} \}, G(i)={V(i),E(i),X(i)}={A(i),X(i)}, Vertive, Edge, Features with includes in XXX, and , where

  • V(i)=TOP(Ri,:,k),\mathcal{V}^{(i)} = \text{TOP}(\mathbf{R}_{i,:}, k),V(i)=TOP(Ri,:​,k),

  • A(i)=AV(i),V(i),X(i)=XV(i),:,\mathbf{A}^{(i)} = \mathbf{A}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}, \mathcal{V}^{(i)}}, \quad \mathbf{X}^{(i)} = \mathbf{X}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}, :}, A(i)=AV(i),V(i)​,X(i)=XV(i),:​,,

2）Counterfactual Data Augmentation

GraphVAG: graph variational auto-encoder
latent embedding H={h1,h2,⋯ ,hk}H=\{h_1, h_2, \cdots, h_k\}H={h1​,h2​,⋯,hk​} HHH is sampled from q(H∣X,A)q(H|X, A)q(H∣X,A), p(𝐻)p(𝐻)p(H) is a standard Normal prior distribution
L=\mathcal{L}=L=

s~i\tilde{s}_is~i​: summary of neighbor info, aggregationof all nodes in subgarph G(i)\mathcal{G}^{(i)}G(i)
s~i=1∣V(i)∣∑j∈V(i)sj\tilde{s}_i = \frac{1}{|\mathcal{V}^{(i)}|} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{V}^{(i)}} s_js~i​=∣V(i)∣1​∑j∈V(i)​sj​

Discriminator,D(⋅)D(\cdot)D(⋅)
D(H,b)D(\mathbf{H}, b)D(H,b) predicts the probability of whether the summary of sensitive attribute values is in range bbb

Fairness Constraint
Ld=∑b∈BE[log⁡(D(H,b))]L_d = \sum_{b \in B} \mathbb{E} [\log(D(\mathbf{H}, b))]Ld​=∑b∈B​E[log(D(H,b))]
LdL_dLd​ is a regularizer to minimize the mutual information between the summary of sensitive attribute values and the
embeddings

Final Loss for Counterfactual Data Augmentation
La=Lr+βLdL_a = L_r + \beta L_dLa​=Lr​+βLd​
β\betaβ is a hyperparameter for the weight of fairness constraint
Use alternating SGD for optimization:

  1. minimize LaL_{a}La​ by fixing the discriminator and updating parameters in other parts;
  2. minimize −La−L_{a}−La​ with respect to the discriminator while other parts fixed.

Self-Perturbation

G‾(i)={GSi←1−si(i)}\overline{\mathcal{G}}^{(i)} = \{ \mathcal{G}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow 1-s_i} \}G​(i)={GSi​←1−si​(i)​} (flipping sensitive feature)

Neighbor-Perturbation

G‾(i)={GS∖i(i)←SMP(SV∖i(i)(i))(i)}\underline{\mathcal{G}}^{(i)} = \left\{ \mathcal{G}^{(i)}_{S^{(i)}_{\setminus i} \leftarrow \text{SMP}(S^{(i)}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}})} \right\}G​(i)=⎩⎪⎨⎪⎧​GS∖i(i)​←SMP(SV∖i(i)​(i)​)(i)​⎭⎪⎬⎪⎫​

subgraph G(i)\mathcal{G}^{(i)}G(i) ego(iii)-center subgraph with noes V(i)\mathcal{V}^{(i)}V(i), exclude node iii: V∖i(i)\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}V∖i(i)​, randomly preterbe the sentsitice value of other nodes: SMP(V∖i(i))SMP(\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i})SMP(V∖i(i)​)

Reconstruction Loss (GraphVAE Module)
Lr=Eq(H∣X,A)[−log⁡(p(X,A∣H,S))]+KL[q(H∣X,A)∥p(H)]L_r = \mathbb{E}_{q(\mathbf{H}|X, A)} \left[ -\log(p(X, A | \mathbf{H}, S)) \right] + \text{KL}[q(\mathbf{H} | X, A) \| p(\mathbf{H})]Lr​=Eq(H∣X,A)​[−log(p(X,A∣H,S))]+KL[q(H∣X,A)∥p(H)]

3) Fair Representation learning

Fairness Loss
Lf=1∣V∣∑i∈V((1−λs)d(zi,zˉi)+λsd(zi,z‾i)), L_f = \frac{1}{|\mathcal{V}|} \sum_{i \in \mathcal{V}} \left( (1 - \lambda_s) d(z_i, \bar{z}_i) + \lambda_s d(z_i, \underline{z}_i) \right), Lf​=∣V∣1​∑i∈V​((1−λs​)d(zi​,zˉi​)+λs​d(zi​,z​i​)),
λs\lambda_sλs​ hyperparam control neig-preturbation weight

Node Representations

  • zi=(ϕ(X(i),A(i)))i, z_i = (\phi(\mathbf{X}^{(i)}, \mathbf{A}^{(i)}))_i, zi​=(ϕ(X(i),A(i)))i​,
  • zˉi=AGG({(ϕ(XSi←1−si(i),ASi←1−si(i)))i}), \bar{z}_i = \text{AGG} \left( \left\{ (\phi(\mathbf{X}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow 1-s_i}, \mathbf{A}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow 1-s_i}))_i \right\} \right), zˉi​=AGG({(ϕ(XSi​←1−si​(i)​,ASi​←1−si​(i)​))i​}),
  • z‾i=AGG({(ϕ(XSi←SMP(SV∖i(i)(i))(i),ASi←SMP(SV∖i(i)(i))(i))i}), \underline{z}_i = \text{AGG} \left( \left\{ (\phi(\mathbf{X}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow \text{SMP}(S^{(i)}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}})}, \mathbf{A}^{(i)}_{S_i \leftarrow \text{SMP}(S^{(i)}_{\mathcal{V}^{(i)}_{\setminus i}})})_i \right\} \right), z​i​=AGG⎝⎜⎛​⎩⎪⎨⎪⎧​(ϕ(XSi​←SMP(SV∖i(i)​(i)​)(i)​,ASi​←SMP(SV∖i(i)​(i)​)(i)​)i​⎭⎪⎬⎪⎫​⎠⎟⎞​,

Prediction Loss
Lp=1n∑i∈[n]l(f(zi),yi),L_p = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i \in [n]} l(f(z_i), y_i),Lp​=n1​∑i∈[n]​l(f(zi​),yi​), lll: could be CE(Cross entropy), f(⋅)f(\cdot)f(⋅) makes predictions for downstream tasks with the representations, i.e.

Overall Loss
L=Lp+λLf+μ∥θ∥2, L = L_p + \lambda L_f + \mu \| \theta \|^2, L=Lp​+λLf​+μ∥θ∥2,

Dataset creation

Sensitive Attributes
Si∼Bernoulli(p),S_i \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p),Si​∼Bernoulli(p), p=0.4p=0.4p=0.4 percent Si=1S_i=1Si​=1

Latent Embeddings
Zi∼N(0,I),Z_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I}),Zi​∼N(0,I),
I\mathbf{I}I identity, dimension of ZiZ_iZi​: ds=50d_s=50ds​=50

Node Features
Xi=S(Zi)+Siv,X_i = \mathcal{S}(Z_i) + S_i \mathbf{v},Xi​=S(Zi​)+Si​v,
sampling operation S(⋅)S(\cdot)S(⋅) select 25 dims from ZiZ_iZi​, v∼N(0,I)\mathbf{v} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I})v∼N(0,I)

Graph Structure
P(Ai,j=1)=σ(cos(Zi,Zj)+a1(Si=Sj)),P(A_{i,j} = 1) = \sigma(\text{cos}(Z_i, Z_j) + a \mathbf{1}(S_i = S_j)),P(Ai,j​=1)=σ(cos(Zi​,Zj​)+a1(Si​=Sj​)),
σ\sigmaσ sigmoid function, 1(Si=Sj)==Si=Sj.α=0.01\mathbf{1}(S_i = S_j)==S_i = S_j. \alpha=0.011(Si​=Sj​)==Si​=Sj​.α=0.01

Node Labels
Yi=B(wZi+ws∑j∈NiSj∣Ni∣),Y_i = \mathcal{B}(w Z_i + w_s \frac{\sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}_i} S_j}{|\mathcal{N}_i|}),Yi​=B(wZi​+ws​∣Ni​∣∑j∈Ni​​Sj​​),
B\mathcal{B}B Bernulli distribution,Ni\mathcal{N}_iNi​ set of neighbors of node i w,wiw, w_iw,wi​ weight vector

Result

Using Synthetic dataset, Bail, Credit

24 Three Revisits to Node-Level Graph Anomaly Detection

Outliers, Message Passing and Hyperbolic Neural Networks

Previous Outlier injection method

G=(V,E,X,y)\mathcal{G}=(\mathcal{V}, \mathcal{E}, X, y)G=(V,E,X,y): vertice set, edge set, attibute matrix, label of class

  • Contextual(cntxt.) outlier injection
    Normalize features xi′=xi∣∣xi∣∣1x_i'=\frac{x_i}{||x_i||_1}xi′​=∣∣xi​∣∣1​xi​​
    Sample ooo nodes from V\mathcal{V}V as Vc\mathcal{V}_cVc​. without replacement
    For node iii in Vc\mathcal{V}_cVc​, sample qqq nodes from Vr=V−Vc\mathcal{V}_r=\mathcal{V}- \mathcal{V}_cVr​=V−Vc​, among them choose the farthest one j=argmaxk(∣∣xi′−xk′∣∣2)j = \text{argmax}_k(||x_i'-x_k'||_2)j=argmaxk​(∣∣xi′​−xk′​∣∣2​) to replace xix_ixi​ with xjx_jxj​.

  • Strctural(stct.) outlier injection
    create ttt groups sized sss with anomalous nodes.
    sample o=t×so=t\times so=t×s from V\mathcal{V}V without replacement
    Then randoms partition into ttt groups.
    Add edges to make them a clique(fully connected), then drop edges with ppp probability

Score function

The farthest node will have large ∣∣x~i∣∣2||\tilde{\mathbf x}_i||_2∣∣x~i​∣∣2​
A structural outlier node iii will have many neighbors leads to large ∣∣a~i∣∣1||\tilde{\mathbf a}_i||_1∣∣a~i​∣∣1​

Score function: scorenorm(i)=α∣∣x~i∣∣2+(1−α)∣∣a~i∣∣1score_{norm}(i)=\alpha||\tilde{\mathbf x}_i||_2+(1-\alpha)||\tilde {\mathbf a}_i||_1scorenorm​(i)=α∣∣x~i​∣∣2​+(1−α)∣∣a~i​∣∣1​, x~i\tilde{\mathbf x}_ix~i​: xix_ixi​ after outlier injection, a~i\tilde{\mathbf a}_ia~i​: aia_iai​ after outlier injection, Aii=1A_{ii}=1Aii​=1
where cntxt OD, α=1\alpha=1α=1, stct OD, α=0\alpha=0α=0 : α\alphaα ratio of two methods

test 1: ROC-AUC
For each dataset, use original dataset v.s. l2-nrom for each xix_ixi​
do anomaly injection. apply GAD Method to get scorenormscore_{norm}scorenorm​

Novel Anomaly injection method

Sum in terms of Dataset

从数据集的角度来说：

FairGAD:

Reddit:

  • 数据来源：Post on politic related subReddit
  • Labelling Y: based on FACTOID(Sakketou et al., 2022), use the num of posted link(left or right)
  • Graph construciton:

CaD-VAE

Causal Disentangled Variational Auto-Encoder for Preference Understanding in Recommendation
Link: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2304.07922

Challenges: inability to disentangle the latent factor
DLR: Disentangled Representation learning
- DEAR: (Disentangled gEnerative cAusal Representation (DEAR)) https://arxiv.org/abs/2010.02637
- CasualVAE: https://doi.org/10.1109/CVPR46437.2021.00947

In Casual Layer: The SCM is

2.1

  • u∈{1,…,U}u \in \{1, \ldots, U\}u∈{1,…,U}: user index

  • i∈{1,…,I}i \in \{1, \ldots, I\}i∈{1,…,I}: item index

  • D=(U,I,X)\mathcal{D}=(U, I, X)D=(U,I,X): dataset

    • For a user uuu, the historical interactions Du={xu,i:xu,i∈{0,1}}D_u = \{x_{u,i} : x_{u,i} \in \{0,1\}\}Du​={xu,i​:xu,i​∈{0,1}} form a multi-hot vector.
    • xu,i=0x_{u,i} = 0xu,i​=0 means no recorded interaction between user uuu and item iii.
    • xu,i=1x_{u,i} = 1xu,i​=1 means an interaction between user uuu and item iii, such as a click.

  • xux_uxu​ denotes all interactions of the user uuu:

xu={xu,i:xu,i=1}x_u = \{x_{u,i} : x_{u,i} = 1\} xu​={xu,i​:xu,i​=1}

  • Users may have diverse interests and interact with items that belong to many high-level concepts, such as preferred film directors, actors, genres, and year of production.

2.2

z=g((I−AT)−1ϵ):=Fα(ϵ)z = g \left( (I - A^T)^{-1} \epsilon \right) := F_\alpha (\epsilon) z=g((I−AT)−1ϵ):=Fα​(ϵ)

  • zzz: causal variable

  • ϵ\epsilonϵ: exogenous variables from a normal distribution - N(0,I)\mathcal{N}(0, I)N(0,I)

  • ggg: nonlinear element-wise transformations

  • α\alphaα: parameters (A,g)(A, g)(A,g).

  • AAA: weighted adjacency matrix: AijA_{ij}Aij​ is non-zero only if [z]i[z]_i[z]i​ is a parent of [z]j[z]_j[z]j​. The binary adjacency matrix IAI_AIA​ indicates where A≠0A \neq 0A​=0

  • To ensure disentanglement, labels of concepts ccc are used as additional information

    • If ggg is invertible, the equation can be rephrased as:

    gi−1(zi)=AiTgi−1(z)+ϵig_i^{-1}(z_i) = A_i^T g_i^{-1}(z) + \epsilon_i gi−1​(zi​)=AiT​gi−1​(z)+ϵi​

    This implies that after a nonlinear transformation ggg, the factors zzz satisfy a linear SCM.

  1. Generative Model Assumption:
    • For a user uuu, the generative model parameterized by θ\thetaθ assumes that the observed data are generated from the following distribution:

      pθ(xu)=Epθ(c)[∬pθ(xu∣ϵ,zu,c)pθ(ϵ,zu∣c)dϵdzu]p_\theta(x_u) = \mathbb{E}_{p_\theta(c)} \left[ \iint p_\theta (x_u | \epsilon, z_u, c) p_\theta (\epsilon, z_u | c) d\epsilon dz_u \right] pθ​(xu​)=Epθ​(c)​[∬pθ​(xu​∣ϵ,zu​,c)pθ​(ϵ,zu​∣c)dϵdzu​]

      Here, xux_uxu​ is the observed data for user uuu, ϵ\epsilonϵ are the exogenous variables, zuz_uzu​ are the latent variables, and ccc are the labels of the concepts.

    https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/stamp/stamp.jsp?tp=&arnumber=9671990

  • Node level
    • attribute masking r∼B(Pn)r \sim \mathcal{B}(P_n)r∼B(Pn​)
    • x~u=xu+r∘δ\tilde{\mathbf{x}}_u = \mathbf{x}_u + \mathbf{r} \circ \deltax~u​=xu​+r∘δ, where δ∈RM\delta \in \mathbb{R}^Mδ∈RM is sampled from a normal distribution.

  • edge level

c

MCCNIFTY 21

Fairness-Aware 21

CAF 23

paper: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2307.04937
code: https://github.com/TimeLovercc/CAF-GNN?tab=readme-ov-file

FairGNN

uses adversarial training to achieve fairness on graphs. It trains the learned representation via an adversary which is optimized to predict the sensitive attribute

EDITS 23

is a pre-processing method for fair graph learning. It aims to debias the input network to remove the sensitive
information in the graph data

Fatra 24

CAGAD 24

https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/stamp/stamp.jsp?tp=&arnumber=10564850
heterophily dominant neighbors: most of its neighbors have different class labels from the target node

  1. GPNN graph pointer nn: detect heter nodes
    composed of encoder and decoder
  2. DDMP (deniosing difussion probabilistic model): translate, create anomaly neigbors for heter nodes
  3. GAT Graph attention network: detect anomaly nodes
    有点想加一个PRAUC的测试指标： 所以当我们希望模型在正负样本上都能表现较好时使用 ROC-AUC 衡量，如果我们只关注模型对正样本的分辨能力使用 PR-AUC 更好

GFCN 24

https://arxiv.org/abs/2010.10274

GAD-NR 24 (in Pygod)

GAD with node rep learn

a survey on GAD -21

method and datasets
https://github.com/XiaoxiaoMa-MQ/Awesome-Deep-Graph-Anomaly-Detection

a survey 23: Graph Learning for Anomaly Analytics: Algorithms, Applications, and Challenges

https://dl.acm.org/doi/full/10.1145/3570906

ADA-GAD 24 AAAI（Anomaly-Denoised Autoencoders for Graph Anomaly Detection）

https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/28691

感觉最后写出来的应该类似是 Improving fairness for node-level GAE based GAD models via disentanglement learning

Domain Adaptation

属于transfer learning
2.2 GDA的两个用途：node/graph classification

  • U∈{S,T}\mathcal{U}\in\{S,T\}U∈{S,T} Domain
  • PU(X,Y)P_{\mathcal{U}}(X,Y)PU​(X,Y): joint feature and label distribution
  • {(xi,yi)}i=1N\{(x_i,y_i)\}_{i=1}^N{(xi​,yi​)}i=1N​: labeled source data
  • {(xi)}i=1M\{(x_i)\}_{i=1}^M{(xi​)}i=1M​: unlabeled target data IID sampled from the source and target domain respectively.
  • ϕ:X→H\phi:\mathcal{X}\rightarrow\mathcal{H}ϕ:X→H: a feature encoder
  • g:H→Yg:\mathcal{H}\rightarrow\mathcal{Y}g:H→Y: a classifier
  • ϵU(g∘ϕ)=PU(g(ϕ(X))≠Y)\epsilon_{\mathcal{U}}(g\circ\phi)=P_{\mathcal{U}}(g(\phi(X))\neq Y)ϵU​(g∘ϕ)=PU​(g(ϕ(X))​=Y):classification error in domain U\mathcal{U}U
  • The objective is to train the model with available data to minimize target error ϵT(g∘ϕ)\epsilon_T(g\circ\phi)ϵT​(g∘ϕ) when predicting target labels.

A popular DA strategy is to learn domain-invariant representation, ensuring similar PS(H)P_S(H)PS​(H) and PT(H)P_T(H)PT​(H) and minimizing the source error ϵS(g∘ϕ)\epsilon_S(g\circ\phi)ϵS​(g∘ϕ) to retain classification capability simultaneously (Zhao et al., 2019). This is achieved through

  • Feature Shift: PS(X∣Y)≠PT(X∣Y)P_S(X|Y) \neq P_T(X|Y)PS​(X∣Y)​=PT​(X∣Y)
    • Assume ndoe feature xux_uxu​，u∈Vu \in \mathcal{V}u∈V are IID sampled from P(X∣Y)P(X|Y)P(X∣Y). Therefore, P(X=x∣Y=y)=∏u∈VP(X=xu∣Y=yu)P(X = x|Y = y) = \prod_{u \in \mathcal{V}} P(X = x_u|Y = y_u)P(X=x∣Y=y)=∏u∈V​P(X=xu​∣Y=yu​)

Preassumption on model:

X←Y→AX\leftarrow Y \rightarrow AX←Y→A. Lables are generated first, then A and X are generated.

  • Strcture Shift: PS(A,Y)≠PT(A,Y)P_S(A, Y) \neq P_T(A, Y)PS​(A,Y)​=PT​(A,Y)
    • Given joint distribution of AAA, and node labels P(A,Y)P(A, Y)P(A,Y)

Preassumption:

  1. Model: X←Y→AX\leftarrow Y \rightarrow AX←Y→A. Lables are generated first, then A and X are generated.
  2. No Feature Shift: PS(X∣Y)=PT(X∣Y)P_S(X|Y) = P_T(X|Y)PS​(X∣Y)=PT​(X∣Y)

Structure Shift: PU(A,Y)=PU(A∣Y)PU(Y)P_{U}(A, Y) = P_{U}(A|Y)P_{U}(Y)PU​(A,Y)=PU​(A∣Y)PU​(Y)

  • Conditional Structure Shift: PS(A∣Y)≠PT(A∣Y)P_S(A|Y) \neq P_T(A|Y)PS​(A∣Y)​=PT​(A∣Y)
  • Label Shift: PS(Y)≠PT(Y)P_S(Y) \neq P_T(Y)PS​(Y)​=PT​(Y)

Because of the interconnected nature of graph data, the IID is not satisfied for strcture shift, and new alogrithm is needed for solving CSS.

structure shift is unique to graphs. In contrast to feature shift, which is analogous to non-IID feature shift in non-graph data, structure shift cannot be solved by adapting traditional conditional shift methods. Therefore, we assume feature shift is resolved, i.e., $P_S(X|Y) = P_T

Even if PS(H(k)∣Y)=PT(H(k)∣Y)P_S(H^{(k)}|Y) = P_T(H^{(k)}|Y)PS​(H(k)∣Y)=PT​(H(k)∣Y)
CSS may lead to PS(H(k+1)∣Y)≠PT(H(k+1)∣Y)P_S(H^{(k+1)}|Y) \neq P_T(H^{(k+1)}|Y)PS​(H(k+1)∣Y)​=PT​(H(k+1)∣Y)

GNN

hu(k+1)=UPT(hu(k),AGG({{hv(k):v∈Nu}}))h_u^{(k+1)} = \text{UPT}\left(h_u^{(k)}, \text{AGG}\left(\{\{h_v^{(k)} : v \in \mathcal{N}_u\}\}\right)\right) hu(k+1)​=UPT(hu(k)​,AGG({{hv(k)​:v∈Nu​}}))

  • {{⋅}}\{\{\cdot\}\}{{⋅}}: Multiset
  • hu(k+1)h_u^{(k+1)}hu(k+1)​: The updated representation of node uuu at layer k+1k+1k+1.
  • AGG(⋅)\text{AGG}(\cdot)AGG(⋅): Aggregates message from neighbors.
  • UPT(⋅)\text{UPT}(\cdot)UPT(⋅): Update function

Theorem 3.3 (Sufficient conditions for addressing CSS).

Given the following assumptions

  • Conditional Alignment in the previous layer k
    • PS(H(k)∣Y)=PT(H(k)∣Y)P_S(H^{(k)}|Y) = P_T(H^{(k)}|Y)PS​(H(k)∣Y)=PT​(H(k)∣Y) and ∀u∈Vu\forall u \in \mathcal{V}_u∀u∈Vu​, given Y=yuY = y_uY=yu​, hu(k)h_u^{(k)}hu(k)​ is independently sampled from PU(H(k)∣Y)P_{\mathcal{U}}(H^{(k)}|Y)PU​(H(k)∣Y).
  • Edge Conditional Independence
    • Given node labels yyy, edges mutually independently exist in the graph.

If there exists a transformation that modifies the neighborhood of node uuu: Nu→N~u,∀u∈VS\mathcal{N}_u \rightarrow \tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u, \forall u \in \mathcal{V}_SNu​→N~u​,∀u∈VS​, such that

  • PS(∣N~u∣∣Yu=i)=PT(∣N~u∣∣Yu=i)P_S(|\tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u||Y_u = i) = P_T(|\tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u||Y_u = i)PS​(∣N~u​∣∣Yu​=i)=PT​(∣N~u​∣∣Yu​=i)
  • PS(Yv∣Yu=i,v∈N~u)=PT(Yv∣Yu=i,v∈Nu),∀i,v∈YP_S(Y_v|Y_u = i, v \in \tilde{\mathcal{N}}_u) = P_T(Y_v|Y_u = i, v \in \mathcal{N}_u), \forall i, v \in \mathcal{Y}PS​(Yv​∣Yu​=i,v∈N~u​)=PT​(Yv​∣Yu​=i,v∈Nu​),∀i,v∈Y

then
PS(H(k+1)∣Y)=PT(H(k+1)∣Y) is satisfiedP_S(H^{(k+1)}|Y) = P_T(H^{(k+1)}|Y) \text{ is satisfied}PS​(H(k+1)∣Y)=PT​(H(k+1)∣Y) is satisfied

ϕγ\phi_\gammaϕγ​: GNN encoding with edge weight adjusting
ϕ\phiϕ: GNN encoding without adjusting
last-layer alignment PS(H(L)∣Y)=PT(H(L)∣Y)P_S(H^{(L)} \mid Y) = P_T(H^{(L)} \mid Y)PS​(H(L)∣Y)=PT​(H(L)∣Y)can be achieved with hS(L)=ϕγ(xS,AS)h_S^{(L)} = \phi_\gamma(x_S, A_S)hS(L)​=ϕγ​(xS​,AS​) and hT(L)=ϕ(xT,AT)h_T^{(L)} = \phi(x_T, A_T)hT(L)​=ϕ(xT​,AT​). Note that based on conditional alignment in the distribution of randomly sampled node representations PS(H(L)∣Y)=PT(H(L)∣Y)P_S(H^{(L)} \mid Y) = P_T(H^{(L)} \mid Y)PS​(H(L)∣Y)=PT​(H(L)∣Y) and under the conditions in Thm 3.3, PS(H(L)∣Y)=PT(H(L)∣Y)P_S(\mathbf{H}^{(L)} \mid Y) = P_T(\mathbf{H}^{(L)} \mid Y)PS​(H(L)∣Y)=PT​(H(L)∣Y) can also be achieved in the matrix form.

Gs=(As,Xs)G_s=(A_s,X_s)Gs​=(As​,Xs​)
Gt=(At,Xt)G_t=(A_t,X_t)Gt​=(At​,Xt​)
g∘ϕγg \circ \phi_\gammag∘ϕγ​
g∘ϕg \circ \phig∘ϕ
Y^s\hat Y_sY^s​
Y^t\hat Y_tY^t​

Drawback of StrucRW

  1. using www instead of γ\gammaγ to reweigt GsG_sGs​
  2. Rough estimation for www
  3. Not considering LS

LLM GAD

problem

GNN缺点：
GNN的message passing会导致N和A趋同，降低识别率
尽管在Heterophilic graph上有改进，但是没有改变single node rep的本质

method

(1) Sequence Construction
(2) Coherence-Aware Rep Computation
(3) Anomaly Detection via LLMs

text coherence
elvalueated by

  • llama 2: LLM model
  • LCD-G: cross-domain coherence eval od sentence

    48,509 normal sequences and 7,108 anomalous sequences

Sequence Construction

Multi sequences for each node by random walk (local)

  • starting from the target node (以target node为中点)
  • iteratively sampling neighboring nodes and their connecting edges. (h:点, e:边, hzhzhzh)

Coherence-Aware Rep Computation

micro

  • AGG info from edges within same sequence

macro

  • holistic edge information within the
    entire graph

AD via LLMs
